answer:
This article will help you troubleshoot invoices that appear to be missing from the portal after retrieval from Krajowy System e-Faktur (KSeF).
It explains the most common causes of missing invoices, the verification steps to perform, and the information required for Technical Support to investigate the issue efficiently.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Preliminary troubleshooting for missing invoices (No KSeF portal access required)
When a supplier reports that an invoice is missing, the first step is to perform basic verification checks directly with the supplier. Most of the supplier's direct AP contacts might not have direct access to the KSeF portal. These first troubleshooting steps will cover this situation.
Look for a KSeF Number:
A successfully cleared invoice in KSeF receives a unique KSeF Number.
A KSeF Number would look like this: 1234567890-20260101-1A2345B00001-12
If the supplier cannot provide a KSeF Number, there is a high chance the invoice was not successfully cleared in KSeF. Without successful clearance, the invoice cannot be retrieved through the KSeF API.
Verify the exact Invoice Number
:
In Poland, suppliers can use special characters and formatting. Checking for an invoice will require the exact same number. Even a small mismatch may prevent you from finding a document correctly.
Please verify the invoice number character-by-character, including:
Dashes (-) vs underscores (_) vs slash (/).
Leading zeros such as 00123456 (If you receive document numbers via Excel, it is possible that Excel automatically removes leading zeros depending on the cell formatting).
Extra spaces.
Prefixes or suffixes.
Date-based document numbers such as 01/04/2026 (If you receive document numbers via Excel, it is possible that Excel automatically transforms the date into a number value like 46113 that represents 01/04/2026).
Verify the Receiver Tax ID
:
Please confirm the supplier used the correct buyer Tax ID when submitting the invoice to KSeF. Common issues include:
Incorrect Tax ID.
Wrong country code or country prefix.
Invoice submitted for another legal entity.
If the invoice was submitted with the wrong receiver Tax ID, the document may be assigned to another entity in KSeF and cannot be retrieved for the intended buyer account.

Step 2:
Direct troubleshooting for missing invoices with the KSeF portal
If the invoice is still missing after the initial verification, the next step is to investigate directly through the KSeF portal.
Verify the invoice exists in KSeF
:
KSeF portal can be accessed from here:
https://ap.ksef.mf.gov.pl/web/login
. If you are unable to log in, please contact KSeF support via:
https://ksef.podatki.gov.pl/kontakt-zadzwon-lub-napisz/
.
Confirm whether the invoice is visible directly in the KSeF portal. You can access documents from the "Lista faktur" menu. Make sure to navigate the different tabs and filter options for an easier search.
If the invoice is not visible there, possible causes include:
The invoice was not successfully cleared.
The invoice was rejected by KSeF.
The wrong buyer Tax ID was used.
The document was assigned to another entity.
The invoice number is different.
Reconcile the KSeF Portal against received documents:
Compare the invoices visible in the KSeF portal against the invoices received on the platform.
Please verify again for possible mismatches, including:
Invoice number formatting differences, missing characters, leading zeros.
Tax ID inconsistencies.
Date mismatches.
Download the KSeF PDF and FA(3) XML
:
If the invoice is visible in the KSeF portal, download:
The KSeF PDF.
The FA(3) XML file.
These documents are essential for troubleshooting because they contain the technical information required to validate:
Exact invoice number formatting.
Supplier and buyer Tax IDs.
KSeF registration details.
Submission metadata.
Retrieval eligibility.
To download the KSeF PDF and KSeF FA(3) XML file:
1. When opening the KSeF link or scanning the KSeF QR code, select the button Przejd do pobierania (Go to Download)
2. Then fill the following fields:
a. Invoice numbers.
b. Buyer Tax ID (You may have to try different combinations between NIP and Number VAT-UE depending on the invoice. Depending on the selection, the Buyer Tax ID would be with or without country prefix code).
c. Total amount due.
3. Click on Weryfikuj dane (Verify data)
4. If everything was entered correctly, you will get a confirmation message. You can select the down arrow to download the KSeF PDF and KSeF FA(3) XML separately.
If you receive an error, you may try again using different values above, based on the KSeF portal table or the PDF received from the supplier.

Step 3:
Escalating to Tungsten Automation Technical Support
If the invoice is still missing after all verification steps, please contact the Technical Support with the required information below.
Mandatory information to provide
:
Receiver Tax ID.
Invoice Number (exact match).
KSeF Number.
KSeF Date.
KSeF PDF.
KSeF FA(3) XML file.
Any error messages received (if applicable).
Important
: Providing only an invoice number or only a KSeF Number is not sufficient. If the required information are not provided, our Technical Support team will not be able to perform a complete investigation. We do not have the possibility to connect to the customer KSeF portal.
Bulk investigations
:
If multiple invoices are missing (for example 20, 50, or 100+ documents), you may initially provide an Excel file instead of individual files. This is acceptable as in this scenario, retrieving a second time these documents from a specific date should fix almost all documents.
The Excel file should contain at minimum:
Invoice Number (exact match).
KSeF Number.
KSeF Date.
Receiver Tax ID (This is particularly important if you have multiple accounts/entities).
Technical Support may still request KSeF PDFs or FA(3) XML files later for unresolved documents.
Why the above information is required
:
Our platform does not connect to KSeF as a buyer through the government portal.
Invoice retrieval is performed exclusively through the KSeF API, which has technical limitations and does not provide direct reconciliation or full document search capabilities.
As we can't connect to the government portal directly on customer accounts, we can't reconcile the documents available there with the KSeF API results. The reconciliation is expected to be completed on the customer side.
The information above is therefore required to:
Validate the invoice correctly.
Confirm retrieval eligibility.
Retry retrieval attempts when possible.
Investigate supplier-side inconsistencies.
Escalate to integration partners when necessary.
The more complete the information provided, the faster and more accurately Technical Support can investigate the issue.

 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources

.

Whenever you encounter a bug on the portal or request a new functionality, the development process will follow a similar path. When Technical Support receives an enhancement request or a bug report, our team first verifies it. If development is required, we will raise an internal product change/development request. Once raised, you will receive confirmation from Technical Support, and the support case will be assigned to Development (status In Development).
What happens next?
Enhancement requests and bug fixes are periodically reviewed by Product Management for possible inclusion in a future major release. Whether they are included, as well as their prioritization and timing, is determined by market needs and the functional or development complexity involved.
Because of this, our Technical Support team may not be able to provide an ETA for your request. Some major changes are announced directly by our Product team on the e-Invoice Connect Product page here:
https://docshield.tungstenautomation.com/eInvoiceConnect/en_US/help/RN/eInvoiceConnect_releasenotes/c_whatsnew.html
Resolved issues are also documented in a separate page. When applicable, our Technical Support team may share a bug ID, which can be searched directly here:
https://docshield.tungstenautomation.com/eInvoiceConnect/en_US/help/RN/eInvoiceConnect_releasenotes/c_resolvedissues.html
However, not all released items are published there, so the best approach is to check the portal regularly for updates.
Please note that the above do not include major incidents or outages. If such a scenario happens, our Service Status page will be updated:
https://status.tungstencloud.com/europe
You can subscribe to receive direct email notification from the top button "
SUBSCRIBE TO UPDATES
". Add your email address and select our Tungsten Automation products.

With Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, it is possible to send and receive invoices directly with a data file. We support many globally recognized formats.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Membership level
Based on your membership level, we can offer different integration options with your invoicing system.
Basic:
Integration with selected systems. Supports standard formats such as PEPPOL BIS and other public formats. Personalized configuration is not available at this membership level. The structure of our public formats must be strictly followed.
Plus / Premium:
Full integration with any system is available. This solution allows you to continue creating e-invoices in the file format your internal system currently exports, as long as it is consistent and contains all mandatory data elements.
The TeC technical team will create a custom map to adapt our system to your file structure and extract the necessary content.

Step 2:
Standard format list
If you are interested in using a standard format, we have prepared a list with some additional information available. Some of these formats might be available only for sending or receiving invoices.
Inbound
(source) invoice format: The format in which an invoice is sent by a supplier and received by e-Invoice Connect before transferring to the recipient (customer).
Outbound
(target) invoice format: The format in which an invoice is sent from e-Invoice Connect to the recipient.
Both ways: Used both as a target and a source format.
e-Invoice Connect converts the sender format to the recipient format using the internal Esxml2 format.
BGC Invoice:
Inbound only. An XML invoice standard. BGC stands for "bank giro credit," referring to cash or checks paid into your bank over the counter. Invoices are sent in this format by a supplier and received by e-Invoice Connect before being forwarded to the customer.
E2B:
Both ways. An XML invoice standard used in Norway. Primarily intended for domestic Norwegian invoicing, with limited support for cross-border use. The format is used as both a target and source. e-Invoice Connect converts the senders format to the recipients format via the internal
Esxml2
format.
Note: This format is mostly replaced by
PEPPOL BIS Billing 3.0
.
Esxml2:
Both ways. An internal format used by e-Invoice Connect and also adopted externally by some customers.
Factur-X:
Inbound only. A Franco-German hybrid e-invoice format (PDF for users, XML data for automation) based on the European Semantic Standard
EN 16931
.
Finvoice 3:
Both ways. A publicly available invoicing standard published by Finnish banks. Also functions as a forwarding service provided by banks and payment institutions. e-Invoice Connect supports version 3.x, in line with the requirements of the Finnish e-invoicing association.
Used as both a source and target format via
Esxml2
.
OIOUBL:
Both ways. A Danish adaptation of the international UBL 2.0 standard from OASIS, customized for local business requirements. Used as both a source and target format. Conversion handled via
Esxml2
.
OIOXML:
Inbound only. An invoice format sent by suppliers and received by e-Invoice Connect before being forwarded to customers.
Note:
This format is deprecated.
PEPPOL BIS Billing 3.0:
Both ways. An international e-invoicing network for secure communication with companies and governments worldwide.
See the chapter
Integration with PEPPOL
for more details.
Used as both a source and target format via
Esxml2
.
Pyramid:
Inbound only. A format in which suppliers send invoices to e-Invoice Connect, which then forwards them to the recipient.
Svefaktura:
Both ways. An XML standard used in Sweden, based on OASIS Universal Business Language (UBL). Enables easy e-invoicing without special agreements.
Accepted by public entities in Sweden, although
PEPPOL BIS Billing 3.0
is preferred.
Several variants exist, including customer-specific versions. Converted via
Esxml2
.
TEAPPSXML:
Both ways. E-Invoice Connect supports
TEAPPSXML 3.0
, an XML-based standard that meets European Standard EN requirements for electronic invoicing.
Used as both a source and target format via
Esxml2
.
XRechnung 2.1:
Both ways. The official standard for electronic invoicing in Germany. Mandatory for sending invoices to German government institutions. Applicable to all EU contractors.
e-Invoice Connect supports commonly used features to enable both sending and receiving invoices in this format.
ZugFERD:
Inbound only. A German hybrid invoice format combining XML data and a traditional PDF layout. Compliant with European Standard
EN 16931
.

In order to be able to send electronic invoices using Invoice Portal you first need to register your company as a member of Invoice Portal. Go to the registration page
https://web.fakturaportalen.se/register
. This is how the registration page looks:
Mandatory fields are marked with a check mark. When the field is filled in the check mark changes colour. There is a question mark next to some fields, pressing this symbol will bring up an explanation of the field.
User information
Email address - Add an existing email address (this email address will be the user name)
Name - Contact person's name
Password - The password need to consist of at least 6 characters
Organization information
When the organization ID is filled in, the rest of the corporate information is populated automatically.
Country The country in which the organization is registered
Organization ID - Swedish organization number for identification of a legal entity.
Use the Invoice Portal
Select what to use the membership for (for instance to send invoices)
Membership level
There are the different levels of membership available. Select the preferred level by selecting the radio button below the list.
Registration code
You may have received a registration code from Kofax or one of our partners, enter it in this field.
Finalize the registration
Terms and conditions for the membership is available in the hyper link (
https://web.fakturaportalen.se/terms.pdf
). Study them carefully and when you are satisfied, press "
Done
".
The membership registration is now done.
After membership registration
When your company is registered an activation email will be sent to the email address used during the registration. In this email there is a link which leads to a membership activation function in Invoice Portal.
When the new member account is activated it is possible to log in using the registered email address and the password you receive.
Read more about Invoice Portal in the online help file:
https://docshield.kofax.com/InvoicePortal/en_US/help/RSI_InvoicePortal_help/c_welcometoinvoiceportal.html

answer:
Question / Problem:
How to reopen a closed Support Case?
Answer / Solution:
Support Cases are closed sometimes automatically by the Support Portal Software. This happens if, after a certain period, no feedback is received from our customer. Closed Cases can be reopened 14 days after that they have been closed. If the Case is older then 14 days, you will have to open a new Case. You can refer to the old Case by the old Case Number.
To reopen a closed Case, you need to login to your Kofax Support Account and click on Cases and then on My Cases.
Here choose "My Closed Cases" from the menu on the left hand side.
Search for the closed Case in question and click on the Case Number. The closed Case will be shown. Click on "Re-open closed Case" to reopen the closed case.

A KSeF token is a secure authorization key that defines who is allowed to access and perform actions in the Polish National e-Invoicing System (KSeF) on behalf of a company, such as submitting, viewing, or downloading invoices.
Tungsten Automation requires this token so we can legally submit invoices to KSeF on your behalf, instead of you having to do it manually. The token grants only the permissions you explicitly approve and can be revoked at any time.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
Official documentation:
The Polish Ministry of Finance has published an
extensive official guide
.
Chapter 2.4
specifically covers the KSeF Token topic:
https://ksef.podatki.gov.pl/media/jzrevse3/podrecznik-ksef-20-cz-i-rozpoczecie-korzystania-z-ksef-20260209.pdf
An official
video guide
is also available:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k-ptf9f3JhM
For any issues related to KSeF tokens, the
KSeF support team
can be contacted by phone, email, or chat via:
https://ksef.podatki.gov.pl/kontakt-zadzwon-lub-napisz/
For your convenience, Tungsten Automation prepared a simplified version below.
Note
: At the time of writing, the KSeF portal is available only in Polish. The wording below may not exactly match your browsers automatic translation.
Step 1: Log in to KSeF
Go to:
https://ap.ksef.mf.gov.pl/web/login
Complete the login process using your chosen authentication method (e.g. Trusted Profile, qualified signature, etc.).
If you are unable to log in, please contact KSeF support via:
https://ksef.podatki.gov.pl/kontakt-zadzwon-lub-napisz/
Step 2: Generate the token
Once logged in, follow these steps:
From the left-hand menu, select 
Tokens
 (
Tokeny
)
Click 
Generate token
 (
Generuj Token
)
Add a
token name
(
Nazwa tokena
) in the textbox, e.g. Token for Tungsten Automation
Select the
permissions
. We recommend marking all boxes. However, if you wish, you can grant specific permissions.
If when submitting an invoice with Tungsten Automation, you receive an error Invalid Token, please ensure that you granted permissions as below image.
Select the button 
Generate token
 (
Generuj Token
).
Step 3: Wait for the token generation
Token generation is usually almost instant. However, it is possible to take a few minutes. You must keep the page open. You can press the link 
Refresh
 (
Odwie
) to regularly check the result.
Step 4: Save the token
After the refresh, if successful, you should get confirmation that the token was generated.
You must copy the token at this step. On the right of the token, select the icon 
Copy
 (
Kopiuj
).
Note: If you closed that page too past, you will need to delete the token and create a new one.

 Guide

Issue Review:
When sending or receiving invoices, you could encounter the error "
At least 1 supported identifier(s) must be provided for Supplier.
".
This error means that the invoice cannot be processed because the supplier has not provided any supported identifiers accepted by the recipient. It is possible that the supplier provided an identifier, but not part of the approved ones by the recipient. The recipient would have to communicate their expectations to suppliers regarding accepted supplier IDs. Suppliers would be recommended to provide as many ID types as possible.
The error can be visible in both "Sent" & "Rejected" menus, depending on whether you are sending or receiving the invoicing document. The error message will remain the same.

Solution:
Your next step will depend on whether you are sending or receiving invoices:
If you are a supplier (sending the invoice
): You should verify the following:
There are many available identifier types. The recipient of your invoice should communicate with you about which ones are accepted. You can add them from the menu "
Settings
" "
Member account
". Then, in the part "
Party identifiers
", add a line by selecting a country, the needed ID type (approved by your client), and the identifier. Press the button "
Add
". Finally, at the bottom, press the button "
Save
".
If you are using integration, meaning sending a data file to generate an invoice, verify that you are providing a supplier ID in a proper mapping format, added the proper tags/segments or entered a proper schemeID.
If you are a customer (receiving the invoice)
: Your options are the following:
Communicate the identifier requirements clearly to your suppliers to ensure compliance. You should precise the exact type of supplier IDs that you are expecting from them.
If you wish to make an exception, you can accept to receive the document without the expected value. From the menu "
Invoice
" "
Rejected
", you can find the invoice in error. After selecting it, you can either press the button "Accept" or "Reject".

 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide

Issue Review:
When sending or receiving invoices, you could encounter the error "
The maximum number of allowances/charges that can be defined: 123 on line 123.
".
This error means that the invoice cannot be processed because the number of allowances/Charges defined on a line exceeds the limit allowed by the recipient. If the error mentions "1 on line", this does mean that the recipient allows only 1 allowance or special charge to be provided on the line. The supplier most likely provided more than that value. If you provide one allowance and one special charge (so one of each), it will count as two allowances/charges. This rule is decided by the recipient, and limitations can vary.
The error can be visible in both "Sent" & "Rejected" menus, depending on whether you are sending or receiving the invoicing document. The error message will remain the same.

Solution:
Your next step will depend on whether you are sending or receiving invoices:
If you are a supplier (sending the invoice
): You should verify the following:
Make sure not to exceed the limit allowed by your recipient. If you are not familiar with this rule, you should contact your client to learn about their validations in place.
If the error mentions only "1 on line X", this means you can provide only 1 special charge/allowance. Providing one line of each will count as two and not be possible.
If you are a customer (receiving the invoice)
: Your options are the following:
Communicate the precise requirements clearly to your suppliers to ensure compliance. You should explain to suppliers how you expect to receive any special scenario, such as transport charges or special charges in your invoices (if applicable).
If you wish to make an exception, you can accept to receive the document without the expected value. From the menu "
Invoice
" "
Rejected
", you can find the invoice in error. After selecting it, you can either press the button "Accept" or "Reject".

Tax category 'Services outside scope of tax' not allowed on line 123.

If you are a supplier (sending the invoice
): You should verify the following:
Make sure that you selected the proper tax rate on your invoice. If 0% is selected, the tax amount should also be 0.
According to your tax rate, you should also select the proper tax category. For example, the tax category "Exempt from Tax" must have a 0% VAT line.
If you are a customer (receiving the invoice)
: Your options are the following:
Verify with your supplier the invoice details. They should be invited to either correct the tax percentage or the tax category.
If you wish to make an exception, you can accept to receive the document without the expected value. From the menu "
Invoice
" "
Rejected
", you can find the invoice in error. After selecting it, you can either press the button "Accept" or "Reject".

 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide

Issue Review:
When sending or receiving invoices, you could encounter multiple errors:
The number of decimals for
Price value
(1333.3333333) on line 1 exceeds the maximum allowed (4).
The number of decimals for
Quantity value
(0.083333) on line 8 exceeds the maximum allowed (3).
The number of decimals for
Unit price basis value
(0.0083333333) on line 1 exceeds the maximum allowed (2).
This error means that the invoice contains numeric values with more decimals than allowed by the recipient. The number of decimal numbers allowed can vary depending on the recipient's rules. The error message will always mention the expected value at the end. For example:
Error message
: The number of decimals for Price value (
1333.3333333
) on line 1 exceeds the maximum allowed (
4
).
Expected value

 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide

Issue Review:
When sending or receiving invoices, you could encounter the error "
Value provided for GTIN on line 1 (123) is invalid.
".
This error indicates that the GTIN (Global Trade Item Number) provided on line 1 is not a valid GTIN. GTINs are unique identifiers used globally to identify products, and they must follow specific formats and validation rules (such as length and check digit).
You can verify GTINs using the GS1 validation service:
https://www.gs1.org/services/verified-by-gs1/results?gtin=
The error can be visible in both "Sent" & "Rejected" menus, depending on whether you are sending or receiving the invoicing document. The error message will remain the same.

Solution:
Your next step will depend on whether you are sending or receiving invoices:
If you are a supplier (sending the invoice
): Please verify the following:
Ensure GTIN validity: Use only GTINs that conform to GS1 standards (typically 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits with a valid check digit).
Avoid placeholder or internal codes: Do not use internal product codes or short numbers that are not registered GTINs.
Use GS1 tools: Validate GTINs using GS1s online tools or APIs before submitting product data or invoices.
If you are a customer (receiving the invoice)
: Your options are the following:
Provide GTIN requirements: Clearly communicate GTIN formatting and validation expectations to your suppliers.
Support suppliers: If a supplier submits an invalid GTIN, provide guidance or direct them to GS1 resources for correction.
If you wish to make an exception, you can accept the document in its current state. From the menu "
Invoice
" "
Rejected
", you can find the invoice in error. After selecting it, you can either press the button "Accept" or "Reject".

 Guide 1: How to send invoices via FTP/SFTP?

Step 1:
Verify your membership level
To send invoices via FTP/SFTP, the minimum membership level is Basic. If your membership level is free, you will need to upgrade it. Otherwise, the features described below will not be available to you. From the portal, check the footer of the page:
Basic/Plus/Premium
: You can continue with this guide. FTP/SFTP is available.
Free

Upgrade

Step 2:
Configure your settings
You can connect your ERP system or an eDelivery operator to start using Tungsten e-Invoice Connect as a hub, transferring invoices to recipients.

Settings

Sending
".
Scroll down to the part "
Integration with a business system for sending
".
Select your business system or format available.
A new section, "
File transport
", will appear. Select Fetch files from "
FTP account
".

Step 3:
Provide FTP or SFTP instructions
After selecting "
FTP account
", you will be invited to provide all the necessary information for us to connect to your server.
Server
: Type the
FTP
server address. This could be the IP address.
Port
: Select the
FTP
port number. Standard is
21
for direct
FTP
connections, and
22
is standard for SSH File Transfer Protocol (S
FTP
).
Username
: Type the username for your
FTP
account.
Password
: Type the password for your
FTP
account.
Path on
FTP
server
: Specify the path to the folder where the files are to be retrieved.
File name pattern
: Specify whether any specific types of invoices are to be retrieved from the folder. For example, to only retrieve XML invoices, type *.xml. If this box is left empty, all files are retrieved.
FTP

: Select a method.
Secure
FTP
/S
FTP
: Select to enable S
FTP
(encryption). This is recommended.
Binary mode
: Select to enable binary mode, which means the transferred data is not converted but sent as a direct stream. If this option is not selected, ASCII transfer is used instead, which means that all data is first converted to text characters and then the data is converted back when it reaches the recipients.
Remove file after polling
: Select to delete files after they are transferred to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect. The user created on your side must have the necessary file permissions for this action.
Passive mode for transfer
: Select to enable passive mode, which allows direct transfer without any further configuration. We recommend that you select this setting.

Step 4:
Final actions
You will need to provide an email for error notification and save your inputs.
Under
Email address to send error notifications to

Save

If you no longer wish to receive invoices but still want to be able to send them via the TeC portal, you can easily adjust this setting from your account.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Stop a specific address, recipient, or profile
With TeC, you can set up multiple recipient addresses and settings. This is useful if your company has multiple branches, departments, or offices. In such cases, you can stop receiving invoices for a particular recipient while keeping the rest of the business unchanged.
From the top menu, select "
Settings
" - "
Addresses
".
Find the recipient profile and select the "
Edit
" icon.
Select the option "
Not yet ready to receive invoices
".
Press "
Save
" at the bottom of the page.
You will have to revert this action by selecting one of the 3 options below to receive invoices via web interface, PDF, or business system integration. This feature is useful if you're performing a system update and need to temporarily pause invoice reception.

Step 2:
Stop the entire Company account
If you want to stop all your recipient profiles, it is easier to make the change at the company level.
From the top menu, select "
Settings
" - "
Member account
".
In the section "Use e-Invoice Connect", select the option "
... to send invoices only
".
Press the button
Save
at the bottom of the page.
With this setting, you will still be able to send invoices, but you will no longer receive any. You can revert this change at any time in the future.
 If you are looking to terminate your account completely rather than just stopping receiving invoices, the process will be different and can't be completed from the settings. You can refer to this article:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (TeC) - How to Unregister or Terminate our company account?

answer:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect supports the ability to add companies to public registries.. If you need to update or remove your company record, please refer to this article.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Information & Disclaimers
With Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, we can add companies to public registries such as PEPPOL, NEA (Sweden), TIEKE (Finland), ELMA (Norway), NemHandel (Denmark), or our own TeC interoperability registry. If, for any reason, you need to update or remove your company from one of these registries, please contact our Technical Support.
The request must be submitted by the company itself; it is not possible to request a change or deletion on a customers behalf.
Partners may only request registry updates for direct customers they represent on the TeC portal.

Step 2:
Contacting our Technical Support
The request must be raised to our Technical Support team. You will need to provide the following information:
Company Name.
Record update or removal (if update, please specify the exact changes required).
Company Identifiers (PEPPOL ID, ORGnr, ORG ID, GLN, OVT, Y-tunnus, CVR, etc.).
Specify the registry to update: PEPPOL, NEA (Sweden), TIEKE (Finland), ELMA (Norway), NemHandel (Denmark), or the TeC interoperability registry.

Important
:
Technical Support for TeC is provided to direct customers with Plus or Premium memberships
. If you have purchased Tungsten e-Invoice Connect through a partner, please contact your partner directly to raise the request on your behalf. You can find more information via this article:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (TeC) - How to contact TeC Technical Support?
 Customers with a Free or Basic membership are not eligible to request a new record to be added to a registry. However, if a record already exists, customers on a Free or Basic membership can still request its deletion using the following contact form:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect Technical Support Contact Form

For various reasons, you might need to test a data file or an invoice. As all tests must be performed in our production environment, please review the recommendations below.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Recommendations
Unfortunately, we do not offer a public testing environment. This is because we do not maintain a one-to-one replica of our production environment. Our testing environment is reserved for internal development and the implementation of specific system integrations. Please note that our Technical Support team will not process files submitted through support cases, since all invoice processing, including test ones, takes place in the production environment.
Sending Invoices:
As a supplier, you will need to find a customer who agrees to receive your test invoices. Once sent, you can review the results via the portal. Because documents cannot be deleted, you and your customer may consider rejecting the test invoice or issuing a credit note of the same value to effectively nullify the total amount due.
Receiving Invoices:
As a customer, you will need a supplier to send you a test invoice. Depending on the test scenario, you may need to provide your supplier with specific instructions for generating the invoice. Once received, you can review the results on the portal. Since documents cannot be deleted, you may choose to reject the test invoice or request a credit note of the same value to offset it.
We understand that some customers may prefer to test invoices or data files without generating an actual invoice on the portal. However, we currently recommend performing complete end-to-end testing directly in our production environment, as this provides better visibility and control over the results.

Tungsten e-Invoice Connect is directly connected to certain VAN operators. This is referred to as interoperability and allows you to send and receive invoices with non-TeC customers.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Information & Disclaimers
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect works with different VAN Operators to extend the list of customers available for you to send invoices to. If your customer is not on Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, but is using one of the VAN Operators listed below, we can add them to the list of your customers on the portal. There are a few important points to be aware of:
You must know the VAN Operator of your customer (how do they receive invoices).
You must verify that the customer isn't already available on the portal. You can do this from the menu "
Invoice
" - "
Create new
" - Then search for a recipient. If they are available, you should try to invoice them directly. If successful, then you can already transact with your customer.
If you get an error "
The electronic recipient could not be identified
", please verify that you provided the correct customer ID or party ID. If you provided the correct one, then an update to our registry might be required. You are most likely looking to change the VAN Operator assigned (as they changed it on their end).
If you get an error "
Incorrect Customer GLN has been used
", please verify that you provided the correct customer ID or party ID. If you provided the correct one, then an update to our registry might be required. You are most likely looking to change the VAN Operator assigned (as they changed it on their end).
If your customer is already on PEPPOL, then you don't need to add them via their VAN Operator. You can already send them invoices from the menu "
Invoice
" - "
Create new
" (but this time select "Search in Peppol Directory"). You can verify this with this guide:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (TeC) - How to check Peppol Customer Registration?
List of
VAN Operators that we can connect
with: Apix Messaging, Basware, Basware, BGC, CGI, Compello AB, Crediflow, EDI Solutions, Exder, Inexchange, LOGIQ AS, Maventa, Nordea, Opentext, Opus Capita, Opus Capita, Pagero, PostNord, Ropo Capital Oy, ScanCloud, Sproom, Swedbank, Tieto, TrueCommerce, Tyringekonsult AB, Visma Proceedo.
 If your customer's VAN operator is not in the list, we will not be able to connect to them. In that case, an alternative channel such as PEPPOL should be used, or the customer should be invited to use TeC directly.

Step 2:
Contacting our Technical Support
The request must be raised to our Technical Support team. You will need to provide the following information:
Company Name
Company Identifiers they use to receive invoices (GLN's, ORG NUMBERS, etc)
VAN Operator name (the company they use to receive invoices)

Important
:
Technical Support for TeC is provided to direct customers with Plus or Premium memberships.
Customers with a Free or Basic membership are not eligible to request changes in the TeC registry (interoperability list). If you have purchased Tungsten e-Invoice Connect through a partner, please contact your partner directly to raise the request on your behalf. You can find more information via this article:
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (TeC) - How to contact TeC Technical Support?

Information & Disclaimers
If your Danish company is not yet registered in NemHandel, and you want to receive invoices (including B2G), then your company needs to be added to the NemHandel registry. There are a few important points to be aware of:
The request must be submitted by the company itself; it is not possible to request registration in the Danish registry on the customer's behalf.

One company account can have multiple users. This allows each employee to log in using their own email address.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Account users menu
The "Account users" page will allow you to manage all your users. This includes granting access, revoking access and changing access. From the top menu, select "
Settings
" - "
Account users
". The next action will depend on whether the email address is already registered as a user.
Button "
Add existing user
": If the email address can already login to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, select this option. The registration process is simplified. The employee might already have access to other company accounts. It is possible for a user to access multiple company accounts. The company account can be selected after logging in or at any time later.
Button "
Add new user
": If the email address is new to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, select this option. This option is for first-time users who are new to our portal. The new user flow will be triggered.
 If you are unsure about the correct option, we recommend using the button "
Add new user
" first. If the email address already exists, you will get an error message when providing the email address. In that case, you will have to use the other option "
Add existing user
".

Step 2:
User settings
After selecting the option "
Add new user
", you will be invited to provide some information and notification settings.
Name
: We recommend adding the first and last name of the user.
Email address:
This will be used for login and notifications.
Phone: Optional.
Mobile phone: Optional.
Fax: Optional.
Administrator
: Select this if the user should have administrator rights. This grants additional privileges such as managing users, editing settings, and updating the company address.
Notifications
: You can choose which notifications the user should receive.
Once ready, press the button "
Save
":
If
successful
, you will get a confirmation message "
The user is added"
.
If
not successful
, you will get an error "

A user with this email address already exists
". In that case, you must create the user using the button "
Add existing user
" instead.
If using the button "
Add existing user
", you will only need to provide the email address. Remaining user information is already available in our system.

Step 3:
Confirming Email Address
In the case you select "
Add new user
", the person will have to confirm their email address. If the user already existed, they will simply get informed via email, without action required.
The new user will need to open the email "Verify your email address for e-Invoice Connect".
Then the user should be clicking on the link available.

Step 4:
Managing existing users
In the future, you might be looking to change user access or configuration. In that case, you will have the following possibilities:
Administrator
and
Notifications
: You can change them directly by using the checkbox.
Revoke
/
Grant
: You can remove access to the account by selecting "Revoke". This will also grey out the administrator option. You can grant back the access in the future.
Delete
: You can delete a user completely from the list. You need to first revoke the access to display this option.

.

Information & Disclaimers
If your Finnish company is not yet registered in TIEKE, we can help you to be registered. There are a few items to be made aware of:
The request must be submitted by the company itself; it is not possible to request registration in the Finnish registry on the customer's behalf.
Being listed in TIEKE does not enable you to receive invoices. TIEKE is not a network for transmitting e-invoices. It functions more like a directory or phone book, listing the e-invoicing addresses and routing information of companies. Invoices are received directly via Tungsten e-Invoice Connect, other VAN operators (via interopability), or PEPPOL.
Similar to the above, being added to TIEKE does not automatically add you to PEPPOL. If you are looking to add your company to Peppol, you must mention it as part of your request.

Company Name
Company Identifiers (Y-tunnus, ORGnr, OVT, GLN, etc.)
If you are providing multiple IDs, specify your primary one.
If you are looking to be added to PEPPOL in addition to TIEKE, clearly mention it in your request.

:
Technical Support for TeC is provided to direct customers with Plus or Premium memberships.

Peppol is a standardized international network that enables businesses and governments to exchange electronic documents like invoices securely and interoperably.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
Table of contents:
Guide 1: With the TeC Portal Recipient Search
Guide 2: With Peppol Directory Website
Guide 3: How to Monitor Peppol Access Point
 Peppol operates through a network of multiple certified Access Points and decentralized directories (SMPs). In some cases, searches may return no results if a participant's metadata is temporarily unavailable (mostly due to their Access Point or SMP experiencing a downtime). In this guide, we will provide two methods to search for customers on the Peppol network, though many other tools are available online. We will also explain how to monitor the status of Access Points.
 Guide 1: With the TeC Portal Recipient Search

Step 1:
Log into the portal
In order to search for customers on Peppol, you need to open our portal and log in.
Access our portal via this link:
https://web.fakturaportalen.se/login
Add your
Email
and
Password
.
Press the button "
Log in
".

Step 2:
Search for your customer
We will use the functionality to create an invoice, as it gives access to the Peppol Directory.
From the top menu, select "
Invoice
" - "
Create new
".
Select the option "
Search in Peppol Directory
". We recommend selecting the checkbox first, as it will clear any existing search criteria from the search bar.
Add the Peppol ID in the search bar. Press the button "
Search
". You can use the participant identifier without the ISO 6523 code before.
If your customer is found in Peppol, you will find them in the results.
If no results are returned, you might confirm the result by checking on our second proposed method below. If you did provide an ISO 6523 code before the participant ID (usually in the form of 4 digits followed by a colon), try removing it from the search, leaving only the participant ID.
 Guide 2: With Peppol Directory Website

Step 1:
Open Peppol Directory website
This Open Source project can allow any visitor to search in the proposed directory. Currently the publication of Peppol participant information in an SMP happens on a voluntary basis. That implies that not all business entities are listed in the Peppol Directory which implies that if your desired partner does not show up in here it doesn't mean that the entity is not in the Peppol network!
Access the Peppol Directory here:
https://directory.peppol.eu/public/locale-en_US/menuitem-search
Add the Peppol ID in the search bar. Press the button "
Search Peppol Directory
". You can use the participant identifier without the ISO 6523 code before.
If your customer is found in Peppol, you will find them in the results.
If no results are again not returned, there is a high chance that your customer isn't yet registered on Peppol. They can contact their VAN Operator in order to make sure to be correctly added. If you know their Access Point, you might verify that their registry is online as per the third guide below.
 Guide 3: How to Monitor Peppol Access Point

Step 1:
Monitor Access Point
Depending on the search tool used, you might face errors when a server system is down. Search tools are actually searching in multiple Peppol access point directories, each of them having their own servers. It's possible that at the time, one of them is not available.
Open the monitoring page:
https://monitor.galaxygw.com/
If a particular server/access point is not available, it will appear in
red
here (you can hover to see the precise date).
If the server is currently down, it will be appearing in
red
(Down) on the right indicator.
You can
select the name of the provider
to get a more proper view of all recent events:

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security feature that adds an extra layer of protection to your accounts by requiring multiple forms of verification, making it much harder for unauthorized users to gain access. While you can continue using the password alone to securely access your e-Invoice Connect account, two-factor authentication significantly improves the security of your account and all the personal information that you store there.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
Table of contents:
Guide 1: Set up your MFA
Guide 2: Reset or Change your MFA
Guide 3: Set MFA as mandatory for all users
 Guide 1: Set up your MFA

Step 1:
Start the configuration
The MFA setup can be started from the
Log in pag
e or from the
Settings page.
Via the Log in page:
If you log in for the first time, after you enter your login credentials and click
Log in
, you are prompted to set up the two-factor authentication. Otherwise, this message will appear every 14 days.
Yes
: Follow the steps in this topic to configure this option. You should select this option.
No
: Continue working without two-factor authentication for the next 14 days.
Cancel
: Redirects you to the login page. When you log in next time, you are prompted to set up two-factor authentication again.
Via the Settings page:
This option is currently not available. You must set up your MFA from the login page when invited.

Step 2:
QR Code scanning
You will be presented with a page containing a QR code and a field to add your six-digit code from the application.
Open your application (such as Microsoft Authenticator, Google Authenticator, LastPass, Authy, or Duo Mobile).
Depending on the application, you will have the possibility to add an account (usually with a + sign) or scan a QR code (with a QR or camera sign).
Scan the QR code
using the camera of your phone.

Step 3:
Provide the six-digit code
Once the scanned QR code is successful, a new authenticator profile should be created. When you open it, you should be presented with a temporary six-digit code. This code should be next to a timer which will be changing automatically.
Copy the code
from your application into the field required from the portal.
Select the button "
Continue
" to confirm your input.
---

Step 4:
Save the recovery code
After entering the six-digit code, the system will generate three personalized recovery codes. You have to save the codes to a secure location on your computer. You may need these codes if you, for any reason, will not be able to use your application. Please note that each line is a different code, making it a total of 3 different codes at your disposal.
Select the
copy icon
to copy your recovery codes.
Save them
somewhere safe on your computer.
Press the button
Done
.

Important
: If you lose access to your application, recovery codes are mandatory to gain back access to your user account. If not saved properly, you can lose access to your user account entirely.
 Guide 2: Reset or Change your MFA

Step 1:
Remove the previous MFA
If you lose access to your application, you will be looking to unlink your user account from it. The easiest option is to attempt a login, and then provide a recovery code.
Log in
using your email and password.
When asked for your MFA code, select the option "
Cannot get a code to enter?
"
In the field, provide
one of your 3 recovery codes
(you should provide only one). Press "
Verify
".
If you still have access to your application, and you can login, you have the possibility to deactivate your current MFA from the portal.
Navigate from "
Settings
" - "
User
"
From the section "
Two-factor authentication application setup
", press the button "
Deactivate
".
Provide the six-digit code from your current application. Press the button "
Done
".

Step 2:
Set a new application or remove MFA
After confirming your recovery code, you will be invited to choose if you want to set a new application. The next steps will be the same as previously shared.
Yes
: Follow the steps in this topic to configure this option.
No
: Continue working without two-factor authentication for the next 14 days.
Cancel
: Redirects you to the login page. When you log in next time, you are prompted to set up two-factor authentication again.
 Guide 3: Set MFA as mandatory for all users

Step 1:
Administrator settings
An administrator has the possibility to set the MFA as mandatory for all users part of a company account. This option can be enabled and removed only by administrators. If you are an administrator, you can follow these steps:
Navigate from the menu "
Settings
" - "
Account users
".
Checkmark the box "
Mandatory two-factor authentication
".
Press the button "
Apply
".
If you have multiple company accounts, repeat on each one of them by changing the member.
When activated, users will all be required to set their MFA on the next login.

To send or receive invoices electronically, you need to register your company in e-Invoice Connect.
 This article specifically applies to Tungsten e-Invoice Connect (formerly known as Invoice Portal). The information provided here does not apply to other Tungsten Automation products. For support or documentation related to other products, please refer to the appropriate resources
here
.
 Guide:

Step 1:
Open the registration page.
Open the link
https://web.fakturaportalen.se/login
Press the button "
Become a member
".

Step 2:
Add user details.
You will have to fill the following fields:
Email address
: specify an email address to be used as the username to log in and (optional) receive notifications.
Name
: specify the name of a contact person for this user account.
Password
: enter a password with a minimum of 12 characters and confirm by entering it once again.
 To create a strong password, see "
Password recommendations
" in Tungsten e-Invoice Connect.
Your first user account in e-Invoice Connect is set up. Later you can add more users with different access levels.

Step 3:
Add organization details.
You will have to fill the following fields:
Country
: select the country of your legal address.
You will not be able to change the country of your legal address but you can set up more recipient offices from different countries and locations later.
Additional IDs
: depending on the country selected, provide the required IDs, such as:
- For
Organization ID
, specify the primary identifier of a legal entity in the country selected.
For example, 656591-0194.
- For
VAT/Tax ID
, specify the identifier for payment of the Value Added Tax and/or other taxes and charges in the country selected.
For example, SE656591019401.
- For
DUNS
, specify the universal organization identification number from Dun & Bradstreet.
For example, 13-783-3910.
- For
GLN
, specify the Global Location Number from GS1.
For example, 4375822721300.
The party identifier is required for all countries. These IDs are required by some eDelivery networks, such as Peppol. A GLN is preferable.
For Denmark, Finland, France, Norway, and Sweden: The country-specific ID field is displayed automatically and is required.
If necessary, later you can set up more recipient offices with additional individual IDs, either official or free-form.
Legal Name
: specify your company Legal name as written in your statutory documents.
Legal Address
: Specify the legal address.
Later you can change your legal address or set up delivery and electronic recipient addresses for offices in various countries.
Your organization details to identify you in e-Invoice Connect are entered. Later you can change the details, supplement member account data and, if necessary, set up more recipient offices with individual receiving settings.

Step 4:
Select member role.
Our member roles are invoice sender, invoice recipient, or both. Under Use e-Invoice Connect, select one of the following options.
...to both send and receive invoices:
Adds you to the list of invoice senders and recipients.
...to send invoices only:
Adds you to the list of invoice senders (suppliers.)
...to receive invoices only:
Adds you to the list of invoice recipients (customers/buyers.)
You have selected whether you send or receive invoices via e-Invoice Connect, or do both. Later you will need to specify sending or receiving settings as necessary. You can change the role at any time.

Step 5:
Select membership level
Tungsten e-Invoice Connect offers four different membership levels. Read the offer and select which subscription fits your invoicing needs best.
Free
Basic
Plus
Premium
 You will be able to change your membership in the future. Upgrading a membership is done instantly. Our Technical Support can be contacted by Plus and Premium membership level.

Step 6:
Finishing the registration
Enter the
registration code
, if any.
Read the Terms and conditions of e-Invoice Connect and select "
We agree to the terms
".
Verify the information you provided and click the button "
Done
".

Step 7:
Confirming the registration
A confirmation email will be sent to you to the specified email address.
Open the confirmation email. Click the
confirmation link.
 The registration is complete and confirmed. Now you can log in and configure your sending or receiving settings.
If you cannot find the confirmation email, check Spam. The email is sent from the mailbox no-reply@invoiceportal.net.

answer:
How UTS attachments are managed in Invoice Portal:
Invoice arrives first - the file references an attachment, IP will wait 72 hours
UTS attachment arrives --> Embed to invoice file and send
No UTS attachment arrives --> Invoice is sent to an error process and message is returned to the sender
UTS attachment arrives late --> Most of the time the invoice is then resent (because of the error sent out earlier) and the attachment is matched and embedded in the resent invoice
If the UTS attachment arrives on its own --> Wait 72 hours to match with an expected invoice,
If an invoice does not arrive and the sender is clear in the UTS file we will send an error message.
Note: In some countries the timing of sending attachments is regulated by law (for instance Portugal = 2 days).

answer:
This is an invoice file with an embedded attachment. In this case the attachment is a TIF image. Most often the attachment is the original invoice.
Sometimes invoices arrive by postal services to a scanning service facility and are forwarded to the recipient in this format. The xml file then contains information about the source of the scanned file, for instance:
<cbc:DocumentTypeCode listAgencyName="ImagingService Scanning">Scanned</cbc:DocumentTypeCode>
The explanation for why the sender is the same as the recipient is (in this example) because the scanning service is configured to work this way, an arrangement between the recipient and the scanning service bureau.
Accessing the embedded file
Look up the invoice in the Invoice > Received, scroll down the invoice page towards the bottom and find "Invoice attachments" there. Selecting the "preview" button will download a copy of the attachment to your computer.
To download the entire invoice package select "Show log information" (below the attachment) and select "Original documents" to download the zip file containing the xml, PDF and the attachment.

answer:
Translation tables applied to outgoing invoices will not solve this issue, since the value need to be adjusted to individual recipients requirements.
The solution in this case is to move the translation off the outgoing/sender stage and in to Invoice Portal recipient layer. The invoice will be validated and a translation (of the unitCode field) will be executed if it is required, but only then. This means the output will match the recipient's format independent of if the sender has used PF/OIOUBL or X8A/PEPPOL.
This validation and translation is currently in place for the unitCode fields and specifically for invoices going into and being sent from Denmark or Danish entities. If other fields should have a similar conflicting format, please contact Kofax.

question: Reference

answer:
Goal
Use the section below to describe the goal of the how-to. You might want to offer prerequisites that must be met before the user begins the task, such as previous how-to's, configuration or product setting requirements.
After completing this how-to you will know how to create an invoice using Invoice Portal.
Use each step section to describe the instructions needed to achieve the goal.
First Step
Begin by selecting Invoice > Create New. You will get to this page:
The first step in creating an invoice is to seek out the recipient in the
Search
field. You can do this by using the search function. We recommend that you search on the recipient's organization number. Searching on the Company name also works.
Here's How it looks when you get a hit on your search:
If This is a recipient that you will invoice often, you can click in the Star icon on the far left. The receiver will then be saved. This will prevent you from having to perform a search on the next billing.
Recipients that you do not get a hit on but who can receive e-invoices
If you do not get a hit on the recipient and you know that they can receive an e-invoice, please contact Kofax or your Kofax partner. Your recipient need to be added to a register with Kofax Invoice Portal. We need to know your recipient's name, corporate identity number and which VAN operator they receive invoices from.
Many recipients choose to use a third-party operator to manage conversion between different formats, communication, security and traffic monitoring. VAN stands for Value Added Network and is an operator of business to business (B2B) traffic. The Operators have interconnection agreements between each other, making mass connection smooth.
Recipients who are unable to receive e-invoices
If you have a customer who is not able to receive e-invoices but who can receive by e-mail (PDF invoice), you can still invoice the customer via the Invoice portal. In order for these receivers to be searchable, the first step is to create a customer card for the recipient.
Here's How to do it: [SEPARATE ARTICLE - add link]
Second Step
To be able to create an invoice for your recipient, select/click the recipient listed when you complete your search, then you will be taken to this page:
Fill in the fields that you need to enter information in. Select the little question marks next to some fields to get an explanation.
Invoice Information and Vendor contact information
Invoice number Fill in your invoice number, both numbers and letters can be used
Invoice Type Select a debit or credit note
Issue Date Automatically filled in when the invoice is created
Currency Currency for the invoice amounts
Language Which language should the invoice fields have
Supplier contact Your contact person for this invoice
Email Address Contact person's email address
Mobile Phone Contact person's mobile number
Buyer's contact information and invoice header information
Buyer contact Your customer's contact person for this invoice
Email Address Customer's contact email address
Mobile Phone Customer's contact person's mobile number
Free Text Space for entering information as free text, a message for the customer on the invoice
Buyer order number Customer's order number for this invoice
Buyers Reference 1 Customer reference regarding this invoice. Some customers require, for example, that you specify a cost center, then it must be stated in this box. If a reference is required, the recipient should inform you of this.
Payment specification
Days left until due date Payment must be made within the number of due dates
Due Date The Payment must be made at the latest on the due date. The Date is filled in automatically depending on how many due dates you specify.
Payment Reference (OCR) A numerical code that facilitates automatic accounting of payments
Payment terms - Your conditions of use for this customer
Delay terms Your terms In case of late payment
Delivery Information
Here you fill in the delivery information. If you click
"More Details"
You can also fill in the shipping address and carrier.
Invoice lines
Above you will find an example of how an Invoice line might look like.
Line The Line number of the invoice line is automatically assigned as a sequential number
Description The Item that you are invoicing
Supplier Art.no Item number of the product/service
Quantity Quantity
Unit For example, if it is an object you invoice, you choose "Pcs". Should you invoice for a service, you can use the number of hours
Price Price in your chosen currency
Unit Price Base The Quantity the price is based on
Discount% If there would be a discount on the item, it is listed here. However, This only works as a ledger for the rebate. Therefore, you need to deduct the amount on the invoice line itself. If it is a 50% discount and the painting really cost 1000 kr you have to indicate that the price is 500 kr.
Line Amount The Invoice portal calculates this using the Quantity * Price
Vat Which VAT that is applicable on the line. If the VAT is 0% then you have to check "Tax exemption"
Tax exemption text If you have checked the " Tax exemption" then you must also indicate the reason for this. A short sentence is Enough.
f you are going to bill for several different articles You can add more lines by clicking
"New line".
Invoice Attachments
This is where it is possible to attach files/documents. Such as for instance a packing slip.
Summary
At the bottom of the page, the invoice is automatically summed up. Here you can also calculate amount rounding by using the
Calculate
button.
Almost done!
Below the invoice summary area you will find these important buttons.
Save If You don't want to send the invoice at once, you can save it. It is stored in Drafts. You can find your draft under
Invoice > Draft
. You can at a later time access the invoice again and send it.
Check Press this button to make Invoice Portal verify that you have filled in the required fields on the invoice.
PDF - By selecting the PDF button, the invoice is saved as a PDF file. This also allows you to preview the invoice before sending it.
Send Select this button to send the invoice to the recipient
Cancel If you do not want to save or send the invoice, click
"Cancel".
Then delete the invoice.
Done!
After you click
Send
Is the invoice sent! If you have any questions or problems, you can always contact support! Contact details can be found on the cover page.
What's Next
Use the section below to describe additional considerations that were omitted for brevity and to guide the reader to further how-to's.

Read more about Recipients:

The sending party (supplier) receives an error message when invoices are stopped due to errors.
When invoices are sent via Peppol all receiving VAN-operators (like Invoice Portal) are obliged to receive the invoice and deliver it to the recipient, in this case the error message is sent to the recipient.
Invoices can not be deleted from Invoice Portal when they have already been exchanged (e.g. sent or received), it is equivalent to a paper invoice and need to remain in the Invoice Portal archive for audit purposes.

not been sent
to a recipient can be deleted. So deletion is possible before sending the invoice.

Easy

answer:
Two scenarios described below "two-factor application missing" and "two-factor application temporarily unavailable".
2FA application not working or missing
If the actual authenticator application has disappeared or the mobile phone is lost/broken.
Start by reinstalling the authentication application, any of the listed in this section of the Invoice Portal help
https://docshield.kofax.com/InvoiceP...ntication.html
Follow these steps to add the Invoice Portal account to the authenticator app.
Log in to Invoice Portal using your username and password.
Since the application providing the temporary two-factor codes is unavailable, select "Cannot get a code to enter".
When prompted, use one of the
recovery codes
created the first time the 2FA was installed to restore the account.
Enter a
recovery code
and press "Verify". Then follow the instructions on the page, e.g. scan the QR code with the authenticator app on your mobile device to create a new account, then enter the code generated by the newly created authenticator account.
Store the new recovery codes safely on your computer.
Make really sure to store the new
recovery codes
in a safe place, press "Done" and the two-factor authentication is now restored.
Please note that the original
recovery codes
expire when one of them is used for restoring the account and they are replaced by three new
recovery codes
. Kofax recommend storing the codes in
more than one
safe place.
2FA temporarily unavailable
If the authenticator application or mobile phone is temporarily unavailable.
Retrieve one of the
recovery codes
you stored when the two-factor application was set up. Please note that each code can only be used once.
Level of Complexity
High
